seoTitle: Add a Link in a Confluence Page (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to add a link in a Confluence page in 2026, using the keyboard shortcut, predictive internal search, and smart card display options.
intro: Messy, unclickable web addresses disrupt a reader's flow. This guide shows how to add clean hyperlinks and smart cards inside a Confluence page in 2026, whether you're linking out to the web or to another internal page.

steps.heading: Highlight your anchor text
steps.body: Click the pencil icon to enter edit mode, then highlight the text you want to turn into a link.

steps.heading: Open the link tool
steps.body: Click the chain link icon in the upper editor toolbar, or press Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) as a shortcut.

steps.heading: Add your destination
steps.body: Paste an external web URL, or type the name of an internal Confluence page to use predictive search.

steps.heading: Choose a display style
steps.body: Use the inline macro bar to switch the link between standard text or a clean icon smart card.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Click Publish to make the connected hyperlink live across your workspace.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to add a link in Confluence?
faq.answer: Highlight your text and press Ctrl+K on Windows or Cmd+K on Mac to instantly open the link input bar.

faq.question: Can I link to another Confluence page without pasting its URL?
faq.answer: Yes, typing the page's name in the link input triggers predictive search to surface and connect internal pages.

faq.question: Can I display a link as a rich preview card instead of plain text?
faq.answer: Yes, the inline macro bar lets you swap a flat text link for a clean button or a high-visibility embedded preview.

faq.question: Should I check external links before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, it's good practice to validate external URLs before hitting publish to keep your documentation clean.
